concerns.text: FR-006 explicitly mandates a 'paired t-test' as the primary statistical method. Task T006 implements 'Wilcoxon signed-rank test' as the primary method and relegates t-test to sensitivity (T025c). This contradicts the functional requirement's specified validation logic, leaving the primary FR-006 unmet by the primary task.

Spec Edge Cases require the system to 'detect' memory usage and 'automatically trigger' a subset sampling strategy. Tasks T014a/b describe implementing the logic and trigger mechanism but lack a specific task to implement the actual runtime detection mechanism (e.g., a memory profiler hook or OS-level signal handler) required to dynamically trigger the sampling during execution.

Spec Edge Cases and FR-001 require the system to 'report' the exclusion count of invalid SMILES. Task T017 adds validation logic but lacks an explicit task to generate a structured exclusion report artifact (e.g., a log file or summary metric) capturing the count/percentage, risking the requirement is only met via transient console logs.

FR-008 and FR-009 require the 'inclusion and utilization' of 'curated' reference sets. Tasks T009a/b cover download and ingestion but omit a task for the 'curation logic' (verifying source literature or ensuring items match 'known reactive' criteria) before ingestion, creating a gap between downloading a file and utilizing a curated set.

SC-006 requires measuring correlation against the external kinetic dataset with a 'mechanistic consistency check' (filtering for specific reaction types). Task T033 implements correlation but lacks an explicit task to document or verify the application of this filtering criteria as a distinct, verifiable step.
